source_pr.title: planner, executor: support index merge's order prop push down at the normal way
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/41028, close https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/issues/43577, close #45387 

Problem Summary:

Previously, we used a hack to support the order prop push-down for index merge.

### What is changed and how it works?

We put the order prop checking at the regular physical property checking. Making it correct for the optimizer to be notified that the order prop is pushed down.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: `executor/test/indexmergereadtest` is marked `flaky=True` and `race="on"` in Bazel BUILD (executor/test/indexmergereadtest/BUILD.bazel)
root_cause_explanation: This PR changes index-merge order property handling and limit pushdown logic, and it adjusts/adds tests under a Bazel target that is flagged `flaky=True`. However, the PR body and the catalog evidence here do not provide a concrete flaky failure signature or a specific root-cause analysis that can be confidently mapped to a taxonomy category (beyond the fact that the suite is quarantined as flaky). To avoid guessing, classify as insufficient evidence while retaining the observed quarantine signal.
fix_pattern: Adjust index-merge order/limit handling and make tests avoid asserting on inherently unstable results when ordering is not guaranteed.

evidence: evidence: race = "on", | maxEle := tk.MustQuery(fmt.Sprintf("select ifnull(max(c), 100) from (select c from t use index(idx3) where (a = %d or b = %d) and c >= %d order by c limit %d) t", valA, valB, valC, offset)).Rows()[0][0] (executor/test/indexmergereadtest/index_merge_reader_test.go)

files: cmd/explaintest/r/index_merge.result, executor/builder.go, executor/index_merge_reader.go, executor/mem_reader.go, executor/test/indexmergereadtest/BUILD.bazel, executor/test/indexmergereadtest/index_merge_reader_test.go, planner/core/casetest/physicalplantest/testdata/plan_suite_in.json, planner/core/casetest/physicalplantest/testdata/plan_suite_out.json, planner/core/exhaust_physical_plans.go, planner/core/find_best_task.go, planner/core/initialize.go, planner/core/integration_test.go, planner/core/issuetest/BUILD.bazel, planner/core/issuetest/main_test.go, planner/core/issuetest/planner_issue_test.go, planner/core/issuetest/testdata/issue_test_in.json, planner/core/issuetest/testdata/issue_test_out.json, planner/core/physical_plans.go, planner/core/resolve_indices.go, planner/core/task.go, util/util.go
